phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: At level 1 the numbers lean your way — paintbrush poke barrages edge out the wall-frost poke control when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: Slight edge at level 2: your paintbrush poke barrages beat Anivia's wall-frost poke control on the margins. Take the short trades you start — just don't force a coin-flip all-in off a small lead.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: At level 3 the numbers lean your way — paintbrush poke barrages edge out the wall-frost poke control when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Slight edge through levels 4-5: your paintbrush poke barrages beat Anivia's wall-frost poke control on the margins. Take the short trades you start — just don't force a coin-flip all-in off a small lead.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: At the level-6 breakpoint the numbers lean his way — the wall-frost poke control out-tick your paintbrush poke barrages in even exchanges. Don't bleed for pride; take the trades his cooldowns hand you.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: Slight deficit on first item: Anivia's wall-frost poke control edge out your paintbrush poke barrages on the margins. You can still trade — just let him start the exchanges and answer on your terms.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: At two-plus items the numbers lean his way — the wall-frost poke control out-tick your paintbrush poke barrages in even exchanges. Don't bleed for pride; take the trades his cooldowns hand you.

phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: QQ out-ranges almost everything mid — paint his last-hits from angles he can't answer. Watch mana; art is expensive.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: The double-paint mark comes online — land any two spells and the passive burst makes the trade real.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: Full palette: EE fear or EQ root into QQ is a genuine kill threat. He has to respect every brush-stroke from here.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Through levels 4-5 your paintbrush poke barrages beat the stardust ramp control in a straight exchange. Play the front foot — make Aurelion Sol answer you, not the other way around.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Your paintbrush poke barrages out-muscle Aurelion Sol's stardust ramp control at the level-6 breakpoint — this window is yours, so force the trades and tax every step toward the wave.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: On first item the numbers lean your way — paintbrush poke barrages edge out the stardust ramp control when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Late Hwei is a fight-opener: R the carry, paint the fear wall behind their front line, and QQ the panic.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: The double-paint mark comes online — land any two spells and the passive burst makes the trade real.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: Full palette: EE fear or EQ root into QQ is a genuine kill threat. He has to respect every brush-stroke from here.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Lost Chapter timing — the palette stops costing you the lane. Poke the wave contact, mark, detonate, reset.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Spiraling Despair online — the teamfight eye. In lane it's the all-in: R-QQ-QE deletes half bars through the slow.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: Blackfire/Luden's spike: every QQ chunks a quarter bar. Shove with QA and rotate — your siege poke wins rivers.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Slight edge at two-plus items: your paintbrush poke barrages beat Lux's snare-shield poke combos on the margins. Take the short trades you start — just don't force a coin-flip all-in off a small lead.

phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: At level 1 the numbers lean your way — paintbrush poke barrages edge out the reflect-barrier poke when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.why: At the level-6 breakpoint the numbers lean your way — paintbrush poke barrages edge out the reflect-barrier poke when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: At level 1 the numbers lean his way — the anti-magic shield trades out-tick your paintbrush poke barrages in even exchanges. Don't bleed for pride; take the trades his cooldowns hand you.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: Slight deficit at level 2: Galio's anti-magic shield trades edge out your paintbrush poke barrages on the margins. You can still trade — just let him start the exchanges and answer on your terms.

phases.why: At level 3 the numbers lean his way — the anti-magic shield trades out-tick your paintbrush poke barrages in even exchanges. Don't bleed for pride; take the trades his cooldowns hand you.

phases.why: At the level-6 breakpoint the numbers lean his way — the anti-magic shield trades out-tick your paintbrush poke barrages in even exchanges. Don't bleed for pride; take the trades his cooldowns hand you.

phases.why: Blackfire/Luden's spike: every QQ chunks a quarter bar. Shove with QA and rotate — your siege poke wins rivers.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Late Hwei is a fight-opener: R the carry, paint the fear wall behind their front line, and QQ the panic.

phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: QQ out-ranges almost everything mid — paint his last-hits from angles he can't answer. Watch mana; art is expensive.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: The double-paint mark comes online — land any two spells and the passive burst makes the trade real.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: Full palette: EE fear or EQ root into QQ is a genuine kill threat. He has to respect every brush-stroke from here.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Lost Chapter timing — the palette stops costing you the lane. Poke the wave contact, mark, detonate, reset.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Spiraling Despair online — the teamfight eye. In lane it's the all-in: R-QQ-QE deletes half bars through the slow.
